title: anthyllis vulneraria f. alba
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria f. alba, also known as White Kidney Vetch, is a flowering plant that belongs to the legume family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which have been utilized to treat various health issues, including skin irritations, wounds, and gastrointestinal problems. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its flavonoids and saponins content, which have been shown to have antioxidant, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and enhanced immune function. In herbal preparations, White Kidney Vetch is often combined with other plants to create teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to address a range of health concerns, from skin issues to digestive problems.
